transcript.clean_text: hello and welcome to highlights of stage 9 of the Tour de France on GCN racing a quick recap of yesterday before we get stuck into today and we saw an incredible display of strength from Thomas to get we spent a total of three seconds of the stage in the peloton and another five hours 15 minutes out front he held off not just the motivated pack but even a late move from thibaut pinot and Julian Ana Philippe consolation for the latter was reclaiming the yellow jersey from the shoulders of Giulio Ciccone today's stage could offer similar racing a slightly shorter than yesterday at 170 k's travelling westerly from sunset en tu glued like yesterday it's also very very hilly first cat climb early on and then to third categories but if you look closely though there is hardly an inch of flat over the whole stage it again looked like one for the breakaway not that yesterday's winner Tom's to Ghent seemed remotely interested in that although data nerds will understand the fatigue in his legs when you hear that he averaged 311 watts for over five hours he had clearly inspired a lot of other riders though a big group of 14 emerged at the front there were no dangerous riders in there for the general classification so the bunch was happy to let them go but that's not to say that they were also Rams far from it there were some seriously strong riders with some big victories among them right there for example yes for Steven Daryl Impey edvald Boasson Hagen touch / Newt Simon Clark Oliver Nathan and Tony Martin mark so yeah from Moby Star defied the roadblock behind and then had a very very tough 20 kilometer chase to get on terms eventually joining up with a breakaway on the first cat climb of the mieux Dexter la tigra newts took maximum points over the top but just behind there was another rider trying to get on terms former world champion Rui Costa had made a similar move to sea air but agonizingly had to give up when in sight of the breakaway and then rejoined the peloton and that's a big shame for him because as you can see from the gap to the bunch it now looks very much like the day's winner would come from these 15 riders per group that size though would never be happy to stay together to contesta finish and firework started with 60 KS to go no one was quite able to get the gap there though and an uneasy truce resumed for another 20 KS when Lucas Passover slipped clear the former national champ of Austria in a stage winner at the Giro d'Italia putting his head down and forged on getting a gap of 40 seconds at one point but there was a whole load of attacking behind in the breakaway sending Bolton Hagen and Tony Martin out the back among several others eventually a group of nascent ratnick say air MP Steven and BER Newt all hit the final three point four kilometer long climb together and then quickly caught and dropped pass over ger touch minuta drove on taking Nico wrote with him and yanked ratnick just behind he was ultimately dropped from them though Daryl Impey looked like he was a little bit cooked but then launches across a yawning gap to bridge almost in an instant to Nico Roche and ties / Newt behind so yeah trap Nick Steven and Nason formed the second group on the road before - ooh - then attacks again taking MP with him but dropping Roche and in spite of their mismatched sprinting ability MP having the edge you'd think they began to work well together to stay clear of even quicker sprinters contained in that second group now back in the peloton let's not forget them local boy Roman bar they did spark some action attacking on the final climb and taking Ritchie Paul and George Bennett with him however team INEOS were quick to spot the danger and snuffed it out back at the sharp end and it looked like we'd have a to up sprint as the gap stayed at around 15 seconds MP was forced to ride on the front for the last couple of kilometres but he kept the pace nice and high Manute launched with 220 meters to go it gets ahead of MP but that is short-lived Daryl Impey kicked and took his maiden Tour de France stage victory he has worn the yellow jersey before but this one would be oh so sweet we don't have to wait long that before the second group arrives and Yant ratnick took the third place finished on the stage just ahead of Mason Steven and just behind Darren every here receiving the plaudits from his team undoubtedly at the biggest moment in was already an incredibly solid career for him and that's got to be a great boost of morale for his mitchelton Scott team as well confirmation then of the results and what is a cracking day of racing at the Tour de France MP from Bennett just ahead of track Nick nation Stephen Roche so yeah and then we have to wait another minute 50 the bunch all rolled in on mass sixteen and a half minutes behind which means that we won't have seen any change in the general classification enough elite then has the yellow jersey for another day ahead of julia Ciccone thibaut pinot best placed of the big hitters behind now tomorrow is going to be a shock for the riders the second Monday of the race is almost always the first rest day but this year they have one more stage to go until they can put their feet up should be too troublesome though 217 case from South floor to Alby undulating not too hilly a perfect day for the breakaway but could also be one for the sprinters perhaps at this stage maybe it'll just depend on tiredness or motivation find out how it pans out by watching live with our mates at Eurosport and also subscribe to GCN racing if you haven't already and hit that Bell icon so you get a notification of our highlights just after the stage finish
